question: Design a `Resource` class that simulates managing a dynamic integer array. The constructor should allocate memory for a specified size, and the destructor should deallocate it. Include methods to `fillArray()` and `displayArray()`. Demonstrate its lifecycle to ensure proper memory management.

test_cases.expected:
--- Starting Program: Correct Resource Management ---

--- 1. Basic Lifecycle ---
Resource created with size 5. Memory allocated at <address_r1>
Array filled with custom sequence (start=0, inc=1).
Array contents (size 5, at <address_r1>): [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]

--- 2. Invalid Size Handling ---
Caught expected error: Array size must be positive.

--- 3. Copy Constructor ---
Resource copy constructed (deep copy) from <address_r1> to <address_r2> with size 5
Array filled with custom sequence (start=10, inc=2).
Array contents (size 5, at <address_r1>): [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
Array contents (size 5, at <address_r2>): [10, 12, 14, 16, 18]

--- 4. Copy Assignment Operator ---
Resource created with size 4. Memory allocated at <address_r3_initial>
Array filled with custom sequence (start=50, inc=5).
Array contents (size 4, at <address_r3_initial>): [50, 55, 60, 65]
Resource copy constructed (deep copy) from <address_r1> to <temp_address_for_copy_assign_param> with size 5
Resource of size 4 at <address_r3_initial> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource copy assigned (copy-and-swap). New object has size 5
Resource of size 5 at <temp_address_for_copy_assign_param> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Array contents (size 5, at <address_r3_new>): [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
Array contents (size 5, at <address_r1>): [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]

--- 5. Move Constructor (Return Value Optimization might apply) ---
Resource created with size 2. Memory allocated at <address_r4>
Array filled with custom sequence (start=200, inc=1).
Array contents (size 2, at <address_r4>): [200, 201]
Resource move constructed. Stole resource from 0x0 to <address_r5> with size 2
Array contents (size 2, at <address_r5>): [200, 201]
r4 after move: size=0
Error: Array not allocated. Cannot display.

--- 6. Move Assignment Operator ---
Resource created with size 3. Memory allocated at <address_r6>
Array filled with custom sequence (start=30, inc=3).
Array contents (size 3, at <address_r6>): [30, 33, 36]
Resource created with size 6. Memory allocated at <address_r7>
Array filled with custom sequence (start=60, inc=6).
Array contents (size 6, at <address_r7>): [60, 66, 72, 78, 84, 90]
Resource of size 6 at <address_r7> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource move assigned. New object has size 3
Array contents (size 3, at <address_r6_moved_to_r7>): [30, 33, 36]
r6 after move assignment: size=0
Error: Array not allocated. Cannot display.

--- 7. Function Call Semantics ---
Resource created with size 2. Memory allocated at <address_r_temp>
Array filled with custom sequence (start=77, inc=7).
Array contents (size 2, at <address_r_temp>): [77, 84]
Inside createAndReturnCopy function.
Resource copy constructed (deep copy) from <address_r_temp> to <address_res_in_func> with size 2
Array filled with custom sequence (start=100, inc=1).
Array contents (size 2, at <address_res_in_func>): [100, 101]
Returning copy from createAndReturnCopy.
Resource move constructed. Stole resource from 0x0 to <address_r_returned> with size 2
Resource of size 2 at <address_res_in_func> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Array contents (size 2, at <address_r_returned>): [100, 101]
Array contents (size 2, at <address_r_temp>): [77, 84]

--- Program Finished ---
Resource of size 2 at <address_r_returned> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 2 at <address_r_temp> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 0 at 0x0 being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 3 at <address_r6_moved_to_r7> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 0 at 0x0 being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 5 at <address_r5> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 5 at <address_r3_new> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 5 at <address_r2> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
Resource of size 5 at <address_r1> being destroyed. Memory deallocated.
